metadata.description: Sanskrit pronunciation guide for TTS engines. Maps IAST and Devanagari to English phonetic equivalents that text-to-speech can pronounce correctly.
metadata.usage: When generating TTS text containing Sanskrit, replace IAST/Devanagari using these mappings BEFORE sending to the TTS engine.

special_characters.ṃ: (anusvara - nasal resonance: 'ng' before k/g, 'n' before t/d, 'm' before p/b, 'ng' elsewhere)
special_characters.ḥ: (visarga - slight 'h' echo of preceding vowel: aḥ = 'aha', iḥ = 'ihi')

om_mani_padme_hum.meaning: Hail to the Jewel in the Lotus (Chenrezig/Avalokiteshvara)
om_mani_padme_hum.notes: The most universal Buddhist mantra. 'Hung' is closer to the Tibetan pronunciation; 'Hoom' is the Sanskrit.

om_tare.meaning: Homage to Tara, swift liberator (Green Tara)
om_tare.notes: Tara's 10-syllable mantra. The 'eh' endings keep syllables distinct for TTS.

namo_amitabha.notes: Used in Pure Land practice. Chinese: Namo Amituo Fo.

medicin_buddha.original: Tadyathā Oṃ Bhaiṣajye Bhaiṣajye Mahābhaiṣajye Bhaiṣajye Rāja Samudgate Svāhā
medicin_buddha.tts_friendly: Tahd-yah-thah Om By-shahj-yay By-shahj-yay Mahah-by-shahj-yay By-shahj-yay Rjah Sah-mood-gah-tay Svah-hah
medicin_buddha.notes: The 'bh' sound is aspirated. 'Bhai' sounds like 'by' in 'bye'.

dzambhala.notes: Used for the Dedication of All Endeavors ritual type. The 'Dz' is pronounced like 'J'.

heart_sutra_mantra.original: Gate Gate Pāragate Pārasaṃgate Bodhi Svāhā
heart_sutra_mantra.tts_friendly: Gah-tay Gah-tay Pah-rah-gah-tay Pah-rah-sahm-gah-tay Boh-dee Svah-hah
heart_sutra_mantra.meaning: Gone, gone, gone beyond, gone utterly beyond, Enlightenment, Hail!
heart_sutra_mantra.notes: The perfection of wisdom mantra. Each 'gate' builds momentum.

om_ah_hum.meaning: Body, Speech, Mind purification (Vajrayana three syllables)
om_ah_hum.notes: Used at the start of Vajrayana practices to purify the three gates.

tara_10_syllable.notes: Same as om_tare above, listed separately for searchability.

bhaiṣajyaguru_7_names.original: Oṃ Namah Bhaiṣajya-guru Vaiḍūrya Prabhā Rājāya Tathāgatāya Arhate Samyak-saṃbuddhāya Tadyathā Oṃ Bhaiṣajye Bhaiṣajye Mahābhaiṣajye Samudgate Svāhā
bhaiṣajyaguru_7_names.tts_friendly: Om Nah-mah By-shahj-yuh Goo-roo Vye-doo-ryah Prah-bhah Rjah-yah Tah-tah-gah-tah-yah Ar-hah-tay Sahm-yahk Sahm-boo-dhah-yah Tahd-yah-thah Om By-shahj-yay By-shahj-yay Mahah-by-shahj-yay Sah-mood-gah-tay Svah-hah
bhaiṣajyaguru_7_names.meaning: Medicine Buddha long mantra with 7 names
bhaiṣajyaguru_7_names.notes: The full form used in Medicine Buddha practice. 'Vaiḍūrya' = lapis lazuli.

pronunciation_rules.rule_1: Long vowels (ā, ī, ū) are held for TWO beats, short vowels (a, i, u) for ONE beat.
pronunciation_rules.rule_2: Aspirated consonants (kh, gh, ch, jh, ṭh, ḍh, th, dh, ph, bh) have a puff of air after them.
pronunciation_rules.rule_3: Retroflex consonants (ṭ, ḍ, ṇ, ṣ) are pronounced with the tongue curled back slightly.
pronunciation_rules.rule_4: The visarga (ḥ) is a faint echo of the preceding vowel.
pronunciation_rules.rule_5: The anusvara (ṃ) becomes the nasal matching the following consonant (ṅk, ñc, ṇṭ, nt, mp).
pronunciation_rules.rule_6: Sandhi (conjunction) rules blend word boundaries. For TTS, it's better to keep words slightly separated.
pronunciation_rules.rule_7: For TTS purposes, simplified phonetic spelling (English-friendly) always works better than strict IAST.

tts_optimization_tips: Replace 'ā' with 'ah' — TTS reads it as a long 'a'
Replace 'ū' with 'oo' — TTS reads it naturally
Replace 'hūṃ' with 'hoom' or 'hung' depending on tradition
Keep syllables separated by spaces for clearer pronunciation
Avoid Devanagari entirely — TTS can't read it
For Chinese mantras, use pinyin with tone marks removed
